title: Edge Hub to Cloud HEC Forwarding Backlog
spl:
index=edge-hub-health sourcetype=edge_hub
| stats latest(backlog_count) as backlog, latest(hec_connected) as connected by host
| where backlog > 10000 OR connected != 1
| table host backlog connected _time
description: When Edge Hub loses connectivity to Splunk, data backs up locally (e.g. 3M sensor points in SQLite). Backlog growth and drain rate indicate risk of data loss and recovery time.
value: When Edge Hub loses connectivity to Splunk, data backs up locally (e.g. 3M sensor points in SQLite). Backlog growth and drain rate indicate risk of data loss and recovery time.
implementation: Edge Hub reports backlog and HEC connection state to edge-hub-health. Ingest and alert when backlog exceeds threshold (e.g. 10K events) or connected=0. Track backlog drain rate after reconnect to estimate catch-up time.
visualization: Gauge (backlog per device), Single value (HEC connected), Line chart (backlog over time).
knownFalsePositives: Forwarding backlogs during WAN blips, cloud maintenance, large burst replays, or HEC token rotation windows.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Edge Hub (system health).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=edge-hub-health sourcetype=edge_hub` (backlog, queue depth).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Edge Hub reports backlog and HEC connection state to edge-hub-health. Ingest and alert when backlog exceeds threshold (e.g. 10K events) or connected=0. Track backlog drain rate after reconnect to estimate catch-up time.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=edge-hub-health sourcetype=edge_hub
| stats latest(backlog_count) as backlog, latest(hec_connected) as connected by host
| where backlog > 10000 OR connected != 1
| table host backlog connected _time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Edge Hub to Cloud HEC Forwarding Backlog** — When Edge Hub loses connectivity to Splunk, data backs up locally (e.g. 3M sensor points in SQLite). Backlog growth and drain rate indicate risk of data loss and recovery time.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=edge-hub-health sourcetype=edge_hub` (backlog, queue depth). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Edge Hub (system health). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: edge-hub-health; **sourcetype**: edge_hub. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=edge-hub-health, sourcetype=edge_hub. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where backlog > 10000 OR connected != 1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Edge Hub to Cloud HEC Forwarding Backlog**): table host backlog connected _time

Step 3 — Validate
Run the search over a health or backlog window you understand, then compare with the Splunk Edge Hub admin views and local health on the edge host. Reconcile queue, backlog, and service fields line by line.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Gauge (backlog per device), Single value (HEC connected), Line chart (backlog over time).
grandmaExplanation: We monitor the edge forwarder backlog so a cloud or link hiccup does not quietly pile up work and age out the data we need.
